test_cases.description: Verify that the provider registry defines a common interface with authorize URL construction, code exchange, and user info extraction methods.
preconditions: Go source tree is compiled with no errors
The provider interface type is defined in the auth package
test_cases.input: Inspect the provider interface type definition
test_cases.expected: The interface declares at least three methods: one for building the authorize URL, one for exchanging an authorization code for tokens, and one for extracting user info from a token
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert interface Provider has method AuthorizeURL(redirectURI string) string
assert interface Provider has method ExchangeCode(code string, redirectURI string) (TokenResponse, error)
assert interface Provider has method GetUserInfo(accessToken string) (UserInfo, error)

test_cases.description: Verify that the GitHub provider ships with built-in default URLs and that config.toml overrides take precedence.
preconditions: GitHub provider is registered in the provider registry
A test config with overridden authorize_url, token_url, and userinfo_url is prepared
test_cases.expected: Without override, GitHub provider uses https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize, https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token, and https://api.github.com/user. With override, the custom URLs are used instead.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
defaultProvider = registry.GetProvider('github')
assert defaultProvider.authorizeURL == 'https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize'
assert defaultProvider.tokenURL == 'https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token'
assert defaultProvider.userInfoURL == 'https://api.github.com/user'

overrideProvider = registry.GetProviderWithConfig('github', config_override)
assert overrideProvider.authorizeURL == 'https://custom.example.com/oauth/authorize'
assert overrideProvider.tokenURL == 'https://custom.example.com/oauth/token'
assert overrideProvider.userInfoURL == 'https://custom.example.com/api/user'

test_cases.description: Verify that a new provider can be registered in the registry without modifying auth middleware or handler code.
preconditions: Provider registry is initialized
A mock provider implementing the Provider interface is prepared

input.mock_provider: implements Provider interface with test URLs and field mappings

test_cases.expected: After registering the mock provider, registry.GetProvider('testprovider') returns the mock provider without any changes to middleware or handler files
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
mockProvider = newMockProvider('testprovider')
registry.Register('testprovider', mockProvider)
retrieved = registry.GetProvider('testprovider')
assert retrieved == mockProvider
assert no middleware or handler files were modified

test_cases.description: Verify that removing a provider from config retains existing user records and API keys while preventing re-authentication via that provider.
preconditions: Database is initialized with schema
A user record exists with provider='github' and a valid provider_id
An API key scoped to that user exists in the api_keys table
The application is restarted with github provider removed from config.toml

input.action: attempt OAuth callback with github provider after it is removed from config

test_cases.expected: User record and API key remain in the database unchanged. POST /api/v1/auth/callback with provider=github returns HTTP 400 with unsupported provider error envelope.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
user = db.QueryUser(provider='github', provider_id='12345')
assert user != nil
assert db.CountAPIKeys(user_id=user.id) > 0

response = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider: 'github', code: 'abc', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost/cb'})
assert response.statusCode == 400
assert response.body.error.code == '400'
assert response.body.error.message contains 'unsupported provider'

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/auth/providers returns an array of configured provider objects with name and authorize_url, without exposing secrets.
preconditions: Echo server is running
GitHub provider is configured in config.toml
No Authorization header required for this endpoint
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON array containing at least one object with 'name' and 'authorize_url' fields. No client_secret, token_url, or credentials fields are present.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/auth/providers')
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert isArray(response.body)
assert response.body[0].name == 'github'
assert response.body[0].authorize_url starts with 'https://github.com'
assert 'client_secret' not in response.body[0]
assert 'token_url' not in response.body[0]

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/auth/callback exchanges the authorization code, upserts the user, and returns the user object.
preconditions: Echo server is running
GitHub provider is configured
Mock HTTP server is set up to respond at token_url and userinfo_url with valid responses
Database is initialized with schema
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with user object containing id, username, email, provider, provider_id, and status=active. User record is created in the database.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider: 'github', code: 'validcode123', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:9999/callback'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body.username != ''
assert response.body.email != ''
assert response.body.provider == 'github'
assert response.body.status == 'active'

dbUser = db.QueryUser(provider='github', provider_id=response.body.provider_id)
assert dbUser != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that OAuth callback for a blocked user returns HTTP 200 with user object but does not change the user status to active.
preconditions: Echo server is running
A user record exists with provider='github', provider_id='blocked_user_001', status='blocked'
Mock HTTP server returns valid token and user info for provider_id='blocked_user_001'
Database is initialized with schema
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with user object where status='blocked'. Database record status remains 'blocked' after the call.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider: 'github', code: 'validcode_blocked', redirect_uri: 'http://localhost:9999/callback'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body.status == 'blocked'

dbUser = db.QueryUser(provider='github', provider_id='blocked_user_001')
assert dbUser.status == 'blocked'

test_cases.description: Verify that auth middleware applies to all /api/v1/* routes except /api/v1/auth/* and health probes.
preconditions: Echo server is running with auth middleware registered on /api/v1 group
Health probe route is registered at /health or similar
Database is initialized
test_cases.expected: GET /api/v1/users returns HTTP 401. GET /api/v1/auth/providers returns HTTP 200. GET /health returns HTTP 200.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r1 = GET('/api/v1/users')
assert r1.statusCode == 401

r2 = GET('/api/v1/auth/providers')
assert r2.statusCode == 200

r3 = GET('/health')
assert r3.statusCode == 200

test_cases.description: Verify that an admin token is hashed with SHA-256, matched against admin_tokens table, and the request context is populated with admin role on success.
preconditions: Database has an admin_tokens record with sha256('af_admin_testtoken') stored
Echo server is running with auth middleware
test_cases.expected: Request proceeds to handler. Context contains user_id, status=active, auth_method=admin.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/users', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_testtoken'})
assert response.statusCode == 200

// verify context values via a test handler that echoes context
ctx = captureContext()
assert ctx.authMethod == 'admin'
assert ctx.userStatus == 'active'

test_cases.description: Verify that an API key token is parsed, key_id looked up, secret verified via SHA-256, and context populated with workspace_id and role.
preconditions: Database has api_keys record with key_id='key001', key_hash=sha256('mysecret'), workspace_id='ws001', role='editor', revoked_at=null, expires_at=null, user_id='user001'
Echo server is running with auth middleware
test_cases.expected: Request proceeds to handler. Context contains user_id='user001', workspace_id='ws001', role='editor', auth_method='api_key'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/keys', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_key001_mysecret'})
assert response.statusCode == 200

ctx = captureContext()
assert ctx.userID == 'user001'
assert ctx.workspaceID == 'ws001'
assert ctx.role == 'editor'
assert ctx.authMethod == 'api_key'

test_cases.description: Verify that a valid token belonging to a blocked user results in HTTP 403 and the handler is never invoked.
preconditions: Database has api_keys record with key_id='key002', key_hash=sha256('secret2'), user_id='user002'
User 'user002' has status='blocked' in the users table
Echo server is running with auth middleware
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with error envelope {"error": {"code": "403", "message": "user is blocked"}}. Handler is not invoked.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/keys', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_key002_secret2'})
assert response.statusCode == 403
assert response.body.error.code == '403'
assert response.body.error.message == 'user is blocked'
assert handlerInvoked == false

test_cases.description: Verify that the RBAC layer correctly defines and enforces three roles: admin (global), editor (read/write per-workspace), and reader (read-only per-workspace).
preconditions: RBAC layer is implemented
Role constants admin, editor, reader are defined
test_cases.input: Inspect role definitions and permission enforcement logic
test_cases.expected: Three distinct role constants exist. Admin bypasses workspace scoping. Editor is permitted on write endpoints within their workspace. Reader is rejected on write endpoints.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert Role('admin') exists
assert Role('editor') exists
assert Role('reader') exists
assert RBAC.IsAllowed('admin', 'POST /api/v1/workspaces') == true
assert RBAC.IsAllowed('editor', 'POST /api/v1/keys') == true
assert RBAC.IsAllowed('reader', 'POST /api/v1/keys') == false

test_cases.description: Verify that a non-admin user attempting an admin-only endpoint receives HTTP 403 before the handler executes.
preconditions: Database has api_keys record for a reader-role user in workspace 'ws001'
Echo server is running with auth middleware and RBAC
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with error envelope {"error": {"code": "403", "message": "insufficient permissions"}}. Handler never executes.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_readerkey_readersecret'}, body={name: 'x', slug: 'x-slug', url: 'https://x.com'})
assert response.statusCode == 403
assert response.body.error.code == '403'
assert response.body.error.message == 'insufficient permissions'
assert workspaceHandlerInvoked == false

test_cases.description: Verify that a non-admin user can update their own full_name but is rejected when attempting to change status.
preconditions: User 'user003' exists with status='active'
API key for 'user003' with role='reader' exists in database
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: First request: HTTP 200 with updated user object containing full_name='New Name'. Second request: HTTP 403 with error envelope.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r1 = PUT('/api/v1/users/user003', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_user003key_secret'}, body={full_name: 'New Name'})
assert r1.statusCode == 200
assert r1.body.full_name == 'New Name'

r2 = PUT('/api/v1/users/user003', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_user003key_secret'}, body={status: 'blocked'})
assert r2.statusCode == 403
assert r2.body.error.code == '403'

test_cases.description: Verify that admin-authenticated requests can access all endpoints across all workspaces regardless of workspace scoping.
preconditions: Database has admin_tokens record for admin token 'af_admin_globaltoken'
Multiple workspaces exist in the database
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: All three requests return HTTP 200 without workspace-scope restrictions.

test_cases.description: Verify that editor API keys are permitted on write endpoints while reader keys are rejected with HTTP 403.
preconditions: Editor API key 'af_editorkey_editorsecret' exists in database for workspace 'ws001'
Reader API key 'af_readerkey_readersecret' exists in database for workspace 'ws001'
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: Editor key: request proceeds (HTTP 201 or business-logic response). Reader key: HTTP 403 with error envelope.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r1 = POST('/api/v1/keys', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_editorkey_editorsecret'}, body={workspace_id: 'ws001', label: 'testkey', expires: 30})
assert r1.statusCode == 201

r2 = POST('/api/v1/keys', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_readerkey_readersecret'}, body={workspace_id: 'ws001', label: 'testkey', expires: 30})
assert r2.statusCode == 403
assert r2.body.error.code == '403'

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/users creates a new user with status=active and returns HTTP 201 with the user object.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
Echo server is running
Database is initialized with schema
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with user object containing id, username='newuser', email='newuser@example.com', provider='github', provider_id='gh_99999', status='active'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/users', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'}, body={username: 'newuser', email: 'newuser@example.com', provider: 'github', provider_id: 'gh_99999'})
assert response.statusCode == 201
assert response.body.username == 'newuser'
assert response.body.email == 'newuser@example.com'
assert response.body.status == 'active'
assert response.body.id != ''

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/users returns an array of all user records with HTTP 200.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
At least two user records exist in the database
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON array of all user objects. Array length matches the number of users in the database.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/users', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert isArray(response.body)
assert len(response.body) == db.CountUsers()

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/users/:id returns the user object including workspace memberships and roles.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
User 'user004' exists with two workspace memberships
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with user object that includes a memberships array with workspace_id and role fields for each membership.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/users/user004', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body.id == 'user004'
assert isArray(response.body.memberships)
assert len(response.body.memberships) == 2
assert response.body.memberships[0].workspace_id != ''
assert response.body.memberships[0].role != ''

test_cases.description: Verify that PUT /api/v1/users/:id with admin credentials updates full_name and/or status and returns the updated user object.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
User 'user005' exists with full_name='Old Name', status='active'
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with user object containing full_name='Updated Name' and status='blocked'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = PUT('/api/v1/users/user005', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'}, body={full_name: 'Updated Name', status: 'blocked'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body.full_name == 'Updated Name'
assert response.body.status == 'blocked'

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/workspaces validates slug and URL formats, creates the workspace, and returns HTTP 201.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
Echo server is running
Database is initialized with schema
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with workspace object containing id, name='My Workspace', slug='my-workspace', url='https://myworkspace.example.com'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'}, body={name: 'My Workspace', slug: 'my-workspace', url: 'https://myworkspace.example.com'})
assert response.statusCode == 201
assert response.body.name == 'My Workspace'
assert response.body.slug == 'my-workspace'
assert response.body.id != ''

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/workspaces excludes archived workspaces by default and includes them when include_archived=true.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
Two active workspaces and one archived workspace exist in the database
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: First request: HTTP 200 with array of 2 workspaces (no archived). Second request: HTTP 200 with array of 3 workspaces.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r1 = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert r1.statusCode == 200
assert len(r1.body) == 2
for ws in r1.body: assert ws.archived == false

r2 = GET('/api/v1/workspaces?include_archived=true', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert r2.statusCode == 200
assert len(r2.body) == 3

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/archive marks an active workspace as archived and returns HTTP 200.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
Active workspace 'ws_active' exists in the database
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with updated workspace object where archived=true (or status indicates archived). Database record reflects archived state.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/ws_active/archive', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body.archived == true OR response.body.status == 'archived'

dbWs = db.QueryWorkspace('ws_active')
assert dbWs.archived == true

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/reactivate marks an archived workspace as active and returns HTTP 200.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
Archived workspace 'ws_archived' exists in the database
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with updated workspace object where archived=false. Database record reflects active state.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/ws_archived/reactivate', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body.archived == false OR response.body.status == 'active'

dbWs = db.QueryWorkspace('ws_archived')
assert dbWs.archived == false

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id on an archived workspace deletes the workspace, its memberships, and scoped API keys, returning HTTP 200.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
Archived workspace 'ws_to_delete' exists with 2 memberships and 3 API keys scoped to it
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with {"message": "workspace deleted"}. Workspace, all 2 memberships, and all 3 API keys are removed from the database.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/ws_to_delete', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body.message == 'workspace deleted'

assert db.QueryWorkspace('ws_to_delete') == nil
assert db.CountMemberships(workspace_id='ws_to_delete') == 0
assert db.CountAPIKeys(workspace_id='ws_to_delete') == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members adds or updates a membership with the given role and returns HTTP 200.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
Workspace 'ws001' exists
User 'user006' exists
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with membership object containing workspace_id='ws001', user_id='user006', role='editor'. Database has the membership record.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/ws001/members', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'}, body={user_id: 'user006', role: 'editor'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body.user_id == 'user006'
assert response.body.role == 'editor'
assert response.body.workspace_id == 'ws001'

dbMembership = db.QueryMembership('ws001', 'user006')
assert dbMembership.role == 'editor'

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members returns all member records for the workspace with HTTP 200.
preconditions: Admin token is valid in database
Workspace 'ws002' exists with 3 member records
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with array of 3 membership objects each containing user_id and role fields.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/ws002/members', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert isArray(response.body)
assert len(response.body) == 3
assert response.body[0].user_id != ''
assert response.body[0].role != ''

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/keys creates an API key with correct format, stores key_hash, sets expires_at, and returns the plaintext key exactly once.
preconditions: Editor API key token exists for user 'user007' in workspace 'ws001'
User 'user007' is a member of 'ws001' with role='editor'
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with JSON containing 'key' field in format 'af_<key_id>_<secret>', 'key_id', 'expires_at' set to ~30 days from now, 'role'='editor'. Database stores key_hash (not plaintext). Key not retrievable again via GET.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/keys', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_editorkey_user007secret'}, body={workspace_id: 'ws001', label: 'mykey', expires: 30})
assert response.statusCode == 201
assert response.body.key matches pattern 'af_[a-z0-9]+_[a-z0-9]+'
assert response.body.key_id != ''
assert response.body.expires_at is approximately now + 30*24h
assert response.body.role == 'editor'

dbKey = db.QueryAPIKey(response.body.key_id)
assert dbKey.key_hash == sha256(extractSecret(response.body.key))
assert dbKey.key_hash != extractSecret(response.body.key)

listResponse = GET('/api/v1/keys', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_editorkey_user007secret'})
for k in listResponse.body: assert k.key == undefined OR k.key == ''

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/keys with admin token returns all API keys across all users including expired ones.
preconditions: Admin token 'af_admin_token' is valid in database
Three API keys exist for different users, one of which is expired
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with array of all 3 API key objects. No plaintext secret fields included. Expired key is included in the list.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/keys', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert isArray(response.body)
assert len(response.body) == 3
for k in response.body:
  assert k.key_hash == undefined OR k.key_hash == ''
  assert k.key == undefined OR k.key == ''

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /api/v1/keys with an API key token returns only the authenticated user's own keys.
preconditions: User 'user007' has 2 API keys; user 'user008' has 1 API key
API key token for user 'user007' is valid
Echo server is running
test_cases.input: GET /api/v1/keys with user007's API key Authorization header
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with array of exactly 2 key objects (user007's keys only, no user008's keys). No plaintext secrets.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/keys', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_user007key_user007secret'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert len(response.body) == 2
for k in response.body:
  assert k.user_id == 'user007'

test_cases.description: Verify that POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh generates a new secret for the same key_id and returns the new full key once.
preconditions: API key 'key_user007_001' exists for user 'user007' with key_hash=sha256('oldsecret')
User 'user007' API key token is valid for authentication
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON containing 'key' in format 'af_key_user007_001_<new_secret>' and 'key_id'='key_user007_001'. Database key_hash is updated to sha256(new_secret). Old secret no longer authenticates.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/keys/key_user007_001/refresh', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_user007key_user007secret'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body.key starts with 'af_key_user007_001_'
assert response.body.key_id == 'key_user007_001'

newSecret = extractSecret(response.body.key)
dbKey = db.QueryAPIKey('key_user007_001')
assert dbKey.key_hash == sha256(newSecret)
assert dbKey.key_hash != sha256('oldsecret')

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id sets revoked_at on the key and returns HTTP 200.
preconditions: API key 'key_user007_002' exists for user 'user007' with revoked_at=null
User 'user007' API key token is valid
Echo server is running
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with {"message": "key revoked"}. Database record for 'key_user007_002' has revoked_at set to a non-null timestamp.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = DELETE('/api/v1/keys/key_user007_002', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_user007key_user007secret'})
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body.message == 'key revoked'

dbKey = db.QueryAPIKey('key_user007_002')
assert dbKey.revoked_at != null

test_cases.description: Verify that all API error responses use the standard error envelope format with correct HTTP status codes.
preconditions: Echo server is running with the error handler registered
A test endpoint that triggers a 404 error is available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with body {"error": {"code": "404", "message": "user not found"}} and Content-Type: application/json.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/users/nonexistent_id', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert response.statusCode == 404
assert response.headers['Content-Type'] contains 'application/json'
assert response.body.error != null
assert response.body.error.code == '404'
assert response.body.error.message != ''

test_cases.description: Verify that the error handler maps all seven defined status codes to their correct meanings.
preconditions: Error handler function is implemented and accessible
Test scenarios for each status code are prepared
test_cases.expected: Each error response has the correct integer HTTP status code and a non-empty descriptive message that matches the PRD-defined meaning.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for (code, trigger) in [(400, malformedRequest), (401, missingToken), (403, insufficientRole), (404, nonExistentResource), (409, duplicateSlug), (413, oversizedBody), (500, dbError)]:
  response = trigger()
  assert response.statusCode == code
  assert response.body.error.code == str(code)

test_cases.description: Verify that 500-level error responses never expose internal error details, stack traces, or database error messages.
preconditions: Echo server is running with the error handler registered
A database error can be simulated (e.g., via mock store returning error)
test_cases.input: Trigger a database error during token lookup to produce HTTP 500
test_cases.expected: HTTP 500 with body exactly {"error": {"code": "500", "message": "internal server error"}}. No stack trace, SQL text, or internal error string in the response body.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
simulateDBError()
response = GET('/api/v1/users', headers={Authorization: 'Bearer af_admin_token'})
assert response.statusCode == 500
assert response.body.error.code == '500'
assert response.body.error.message == 'internal server error'
assert 'sql' not in str(response.body).lower()
assert 'stack' not in str(response.body).lower()
assert 'panic' not in str(response.body).lower()

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/api.md exists and contains every endpoint with method, path, auth requirements, request/response examples, status codes, and error format.
preconditions: Repository is checked out
docs/api.md has been generated or written
test_cases.expected: File exists. Contains sections for all endpoints listed in the PRD. Each section has: method (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE), path, auth requirements, JSON request body example, JSON response body example, list of status codes, and error format example.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('docs/api.md')
assert content != ''
for endpoint in ALL_ENDPOINTS:
  assert endpoint.method in content
  assert endpoint.path in content
assert 'Authorization' in content
assert 'error' in content
assert '400' in content AND '401' in content AND '403' in content AND '404' in content AND '409' in content AND '500' in content

test_cases.description: Verify that README.md contains a link to docs/api.md.
preconditions: Repository is checked out
README.md and docs/api.md both exist
test_cases.expected: README.md contains a markdown hyperlink or reference to docs/api.md.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile('README.md')
assert 'docs/api.md' in content

property_tests.description: For any admin token verification attempt, the system only compares SHA-256 hashes and never stores or returns plaintext tokens.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate arbitrary admin token strings prefixed 'af_admin_'; submit as Authorization header; intercept all database queries and HTTP responses
property_tests.invariant_check:
assert db.WrittenValues() does not contain plaintext token
assert all response bodies do not contain the plaintext token string
assert db.AdminTokensTable() only contains 64-char hex strings (SHA-256 digests)
assert tokenSentToHashFn == originalToken AND tokenStoredInDB != originalToken

property_tests.description: For any API key creation or refresh operation, the plaintext key is returned exactly once in the response and is never retrievable again.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate arbitrary valid workspace_id/label/expires combinations; create or refresh keys; then issue GET /api/v1/keys for the same user
property_tests.invariant_check:
let createResponse = POST('/api/v1/keys', ...)
assert createResponse.body.key matches 'af_.*_.*'
assert db.QueryAPIKey(createResponse.body.key_id).key_hash != extractSecret(createResponse.body.key)
assert db.QueryAPIKey(createResponse.body.key_id).key_hash == sha256(extractSecret(createResponse.body.key))
let listResponse = GET('/api/v1/keys', ...)
for k in listResponse.body: assert k.key == undefined OR k.key == ''
assert count of times plaintext appears in all subsequent GET responses == 0

property_tests.description: For any request from a user with status=blocked, the auth middleware always returns HTTP 403 regardless of token type or token validity.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate any valid admin token or API key belonging to a user with status='blocked'; submit requests to any protected endpoint
property_tests.invariant_check:
for each (tokenType, token) in [(adminToken, blockedUserAdminToken), (apiKey, blockedUserAPIKey)]:
  response = GET(anyProtectedEndpoint, headers={Authorization: 'Bearer ' + token})
  assert response.statusCode == 403
  assert response.body.error.message == 'user is blocked'
  assert handlerInvoked == false

property_tests.description: For any DELETE request on an archived workspace, either all cascade deletions succeed atomically or none are committed — there is no partial state.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate workspaces with varying numbers of memberships (0..10) and API keys (0..10); simulate random database failure at each step of the cascade deletion
property_tests.invariant_check:
for each failure point in [beforeMembershipDelete, afterMembershipDelete, beforeAPIKeyDelete, afterAPIKeyDelete]:
  simulateFailureAt(failure_point)
  response = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/{id}', ...)
  if response.statusCode == 500:
    assert db.QueryWorkspace(id) != nil
    assert db.CountMemberships(workspace_id=id) == initialMembershipCount
    assert db.CountAPIKeys(workspace_id=id) == initialAPIKeyCount
  elif response.statusCode == 200:
    assert db.QueryWorkspace(id) == nil
    assert db.CountMemberships(workspace_id=id) == 0
    assert db.CountAPIKeys(workspace_id=id) == 0

property_tests.description: For any OAuth callback where the user's status is blocked, the upsert operation never changes status from blocked to active.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate arbitrary OAuth callback payloads for users that exist in the database with status='blocked'; submit to POST /api/v1/auth/callback with mock identity provider responses
property_tests.invariant_check:
statusBefore = db.QueryUser(provider, provider_id).status
assert statusBefore == 'blocked'

POST('/api/v1/auth/callback', {provider, code, redirect_uri})

statusAfter = db.QueryUser(provider, provider_id).status
assert statusAfter == 'blocked'
assert statusAfter != 'active'

property_tests.description: For any workspace creation request, both name and slug must be globally unique; a conflict on either field causes HTTP 409 and no record is created.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate workspace creation requests where (a) name conflicts with an existing workspace, (b) slug conflicts, or (c) both conflict; also generate non-conflicting requests
property_tests.invariant_check:
countBefore = db.CountWorkspaces()
for conflictingBody in [nameDuplicate, slugDuplicate, bothDuplicate]:
  response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body=conflictingBody)
  assert response.statusCode == 409
  assert db.CountWorkspaces() == countBefore

for uniqueBody in [uniqueNameAndSlug]:
  response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body=uniqueBody)
  assert response.statusCode == 201
  assert db.CountWorkspaces() == countBefore + 1

property_tests.description: For any 5xx error response, the body contains only the standard error envelope with message 'internal server error' — no stack traces, query text, or database error strings.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: generate various internal errors by mocking the store layer to return different Go error types (sql.ErrConnDone, context.DeadlineExceeded, arbitrary errors); trigger these from any endpoint
property_tests.invariant_check:
for each simulatedError in allInternalErrors:
  mockStore.setNextError(simulatedError)
  response = anyProtectedEndpointRequest()
  if response.statusCode >= 500:
    assert response.body.error.code == '500'
    assert response.body.error.message == 'internal server error'
    assert simulatedError.Error() not in str(response.body)
    assert 'goroutine' not in str(response.body)
    assert 'runtime error' not in str(response.body)
    assert '.go:' not in str(response.body)

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for OAuth login creating a new user: discover providers, exchange code, and verify user record is created in the database.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run GET /api/v1/auth/providers, then POST /api/v1/auth/callback with a valid code from a mock GitHub identity provider
expected_effects: GET /api/v1/auth/providers returns HTTP 200 with array containing github provider object with name and authorize_url
POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns HTTP 200 with user object containing username, email, provider='github', status='active'
A new user record with matching provider and provider_id exists in the database
No secrets or tokens are exposed in the response bodies

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for admin creating a workspace and assigning a member via the REST API.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /api/v1/workspaces with admin token, then POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members with user_id and role
expected_effects: Auth middleware validates admin token SHA-256 hash against admin_tokens table and populates context
RBAC layer permits admin on POST /api/v1/workspaces
POST /api/v1/workspaces returns HTTP 201 with workspace object containing valid id, name, slug
POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members returns HTTP 200 with membership object containing user_id and role
Workspace and membership records exist in the database after both calls

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for an editor creating an API key and using it to authenticate a subsequent request.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /api/v1/keys with editor API key token, then use the returned plaintext key to authenticate another request
expected_effects: Auth middleware parses af_<key_id>_<secret> format, verifies SHA-256 hash, and populates context with editor role
RBAC permits editor on POST /api/v1/keys
POST /api/v1/keys returns HTTP 201 with plaintext key in af_<key_id>_<secret> format and expires_at ~30 days from now
Database api_keys record has key_hash = sha256(secret) and revoked_at = null
Subsequent request using the new key returns HTTP 200 (authenticated successfully)

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test verifying that a blocked user with a valid API key is rejected at the middleware layer with HTTP 403.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send a GET /api/v1/keys request using a valid API key belonging to a user with status='blocked'
expected_effects: Auth middleware validates the API key record (key_id found, hash matches, not revoked, not expired)
Auth middleware looks up user status and finds status='blocked'
Response is HTTP 403 with {"error": {"code": "403", "message": "user is blocked"}}
The GET /api/v1/keys handler function is never invoked

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for admin archiving and then deleting a workspace, verifying cascade deletion of memberships and API keys.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/archive with admin token, then DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id with admin token
expected_effects: POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/archive returns HTTP 200 with workspace object showing archived state
DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id returns HTTP 200 with {"message": "workspace deleted"}
Workspace record no longer exists in the database
All memberships scoped to the workspace are deleted from the database
All API keys scoped to the workspace are deleted from the database
Both operations are committed atomically with no partial state

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for a non-admin user updating their own full_name via PUT /api/v1/users/:id.
smoke_tests.trigger: PUT /api/v1/users/:id (own ID) with {"full_name": "New Name"} using a reader API key token
expected_effects: Auth middleware validates the reader API key and confirms user status is active
RBAC layer detects the :id matches the authenticated user's own ID and permits full_name update
PUT returns HTTP 200 with updated user object where full_name='New Name'
Database user record reflects the updated full_name
User status remains unchanged
